title: Leg Swelling After Surgery - DVT
chief_complaint: 60-year-old female 5 days post-total hip replacement with acute left leg swelling, pain, and warmth. No chest pain or dyspnea.

physical_exam.legs: Left calf swelling 3cm > right, tenderness, warmth, Homan sign positive

hidden_data.doppler_us: Non-compressible thrombus in left popliteal and peroneal veins

key_learning_points: Orthopedic surgery = high risk for VTE

patient_responses.surgery: I had a hip replacement 5 days ago
patient_responses.leg: My left calf is swollen and tender

feedback.incorrect_message: This is DVT. Risk factors + positive Doppler confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Provoked DVT: Treat with DOAC (rivaroxaban or apixaban) for 3 months. No LMWH bridge needed with DOACs. Compression stockings for symptoms. Early mobilization. Good prognosis with treatment.
